programmes.show_description: A correction officer is accused of damaging his ex-girlfriend's motorcycle. Ex-lovers fight over harassment charges and the installation of car speakers. S10, Ep251
A woman, who has custody of her stepson's child, sues him for living expenses. An 8-year-old girl is accused of pulling braids out of another child's head. S10, Ep252
A painter complains that his ability to perform his job was restricted due to the proximity of dialysis patients. S23, Ep216
A man accuses a mechanic of defrauding him when he failed to put original parts as allegedly promised on his racing motorcycle. S23, Ep217
A man loses visitation rights when his ex-wife calls CPS; he sues for defamation claiming she said he tried to kill her and beat his daughter. S23, Ep218
When a man files for bankruptcy, he leaves his ex-wife holding the bills; the Judge calls into question his child support efforts. S23, Ep219
Judge Judy learns the ins-and-outs of an online 'Gifting Group' then warns viewers to double-check for legitimacy when someone pulls at your heartstrings. S23, Ep220
A brave neighbour comes to the aid of an Abyssinian cat who is accused of attacking a large dog; the cat does not survive the skirmish. S23, Ep221
After briefly dating a co-worker, an insurance saleswoman goes after him for a series of alleged loans used to help him on business road trips. S23, Ep222
A woman claims she loaned her out-of-state boyfriend thousands of dollars so he could move to be with her. S10, Ep246
The cost of long-distance calls destroys a friendship between women who met online. College students dispute their rental agreement. S10, Ep247
When the skull of a woman is found in a Texas canyon, investigators discover that she was stabbed. Can they track down the killer? S10, Ep6
When a woman went missing on holiday in Key West, Florida, all police found was her shoes. Video evidence suggested there might be a killer afoot. S10, Ep7
Innovative, value-for-money products brought directly to you at home. Shop 'til you drop whilst avoiding the hustle and bustle of the High Street.
In the US, a missing woman and her money starts a huge police investigation. In the UK, the shocking murder of a woman in her own home leads to a nationwide manhunt. S2, Ep5
In the US, a grandmother goes missing. Surveillance footage captures last movements of missing woman. S2, Ep6
A wealthy lawyer shoots his wife from the backseat of a car - was it a tragic accident or cold-blooded murder? S30, Ep21
The case of a murdered elderly couple goes stale until the old woman's necklace is miraculously discovered in a pawn shop. S7, Ep28
The body of Jessica Knott is meticulously analysed and evidence reveals a startling truth about the killer. S7, Ep29
When a young woman vanishes, police suspect a man with a history of assault. But can they make a conviction? S7, Ep30
Investigators are flummoxed after a middle-aged woman is found dead in her home. The ex-wife of her husband could hold the key... S7, Ep31
In Colorado, a man claims his brother has murdered his girlfriend. In Michigan, a young couple is found dead in their garden shed. S2, Ep7
In Florida, the body of a teen girl is discovered. The police turn to the neighbourhood cameras. In Colorado, the last man to see a missing woman professes his innocence. S2, Ep8
A young mother calls 911 claiming she cut her own throat in front of her ex-husband and child. Doctors say there's no way she did it to herself. S30, Ep22
When a man is murdered in a bomb blast, investigators sift through the remains to hone in on the bomber. Tiny fragments of plastic hold the key to the case. S10, Ep10
A strategically placed plastic bag found near a suffocated woman made her death look accidental. Further investigation revealed the scene was staged. S10, Ep11
Police search for a missing boy and find his backpack five miles from his home, then his body is found over 50 miles away. But can they track down the killer? S10, Ep12
When police arrive at the scene of a car accident, the bloody mess they find leads them to suspect foul play, but the coroner rules it as an accidental death. S10, Ep13
When a dance business does not turn a profit, the company owner is accused of skipping out on leased space payments; she countersues to take the dance floor with her. S23, Ep223
An enterprising young man sues his mother and sister for alleged fraud and misappropriation of funds after they go into the construction business together. S23, Ep224
A woman suffers pregnancy distress after a heated argument with her boyfriend but tries to rescue her dog anyway. S23, Ep225
Unauthorized Kindle charges fuel a heated battle of words between brother and sister; alleged threats of arrest and death come into play. S23, Ep226
A man shells out thousands to his ex-wife to get her to sign off on his life insurance policy; she claims he shorted her by 5K. S23, Ep227
A contractor claims he successfully finished a friend's bathroom remodel but then lost revenue due to slander about the quality of his work. S23, Ep228
A young woman, who had no right to be on the road, causes a collision that lands her boyfriend in the hospital and rehab for over two months. S23, Ep229
A woman sues her ex-fiance for the cost of taking care of his teenage children. Feuding neighbours fight over damages to a Koi pond allegedly caused by a German Shepherd. S10, Ep253
Single parents dispute unpaid rent and a stolen ring. Ex-friends fight over furniture after a failed attempt at living together. S10, Ep254
A business owner claims he lost money after a large sign, advertising his services, was illegally removed from his property. S10, Ep255
A woman claims she was wrongfully evicted and that her property was held hostage. A man sues a drunk driver for crashing into and totalling his parked car. S10, Ep256
Ex-friends fight over belongings left in a car when it was repossessed. High school friends move in together but fight over rent when one girl moves out. S10, Ep257
A woman claims her prized dog was unable to perform after being razor burned by a groomer and sues for the cost of lost stud fees. S10, Ep258
Unwed parents dispute payment of collect calls from prison. After a series of restraining orders, ex-lovers fight over unpaid rent. S10, Ep259
When a dance business does not turn a profit, the company owner is accused of skipping out on leased space payments; she countersues to take the dance floor with her. S23, Ep223

A bloody trail leads investigators to a dead end at the scene of a bloody murder. Years later, advances in forensic techniques help find the killer. S7, Ep32
A gunman killed a worker at a manufacturing plant. He hoped to have covered his tracks, but forensics enabled police to track him down. S7, Ep33
Find out how police tracked down a killer using a computer, after receiving an anonymous letter about the deaths of several prostitutes. S7, Ep35
A woman recognised a detailed drawing of a serial rapist. Police closed in on the suspect and used forensic evidence to bring him down. S7, Ep36
In the UK, a woman is found murdered after reporting stalking. In Texas, a woman is found shot dead outside her home. S2, Ep9
In Texas, surveillance catches a killer out. In Washington State, a man's vlogs reveal his dark plans. S2, Ep10
Lorenzen Wright was a superstar player in the NBA and a hometown hero. That ended in July 2010 during a call to 911. The chilling recording captured his dying words. S30, Ep23
The decomposed bodies of two victims are found in an irrigation canal in Bakersfield, California, but how can a killer be caught if evidence is washed away? S10, Ep14
Behavioural profiling can be useful to catch a killer, but it can also be misleading, as police found out in one particular case. DNA put them back on track. S10, Ep15
When a man found his wife covered in blood, he was too late to revive her. Police believed blood spatter evidence didn't match his story. S10, Ep16
Police initially thought a California teenager had run away, until they found her naked battered body in a ditch. Will they find the killer? S10, Ep17
A young man refuses to pay what he allegedly owes when he discovers he was subleasing instead of leasing; his sublessors say he stole a TV off the wall. S23, Ep230
'Mr. Incredible' and 'Cookie' make an appearance as a show of support for their owner who alleged violates a lease by owning a third dog. S23, Ep231
A lethal judicial combination escalates when two young boys go for a joy ride that ends in a collision. S23, Ep232
A teenager, rushing to her therapy appointment, witnesses her boyfriend initiate a violent attack on a slower driver's car. S23, Ep233
While on a walk with a non-verbal patient, a woman claims she was bitten by a guard dog who was allegedly going for her client. S23, Ep234
A dog care provider accuses a pet owner of dropping off her dogs and not returning for six months; the pet owner says she neglected her animals and harassed her. S23, Ep235
The Judge reprimands ex-lovers for allegedly using claims of child abuse to get even with each other. S23, Ep236
A student denies stealing her ex-stepmother's identity and sues for damages from allegedly false charges. S10, Ep260
Lying teenagers, rocks thrown at cars from a bridge, and felony charges come into play when $7,000 in restitution fees is disputed by angry parents. S18, Ep1
Two young women attending a video game convention claim they were forced to sleep in their car after seven men showed up in the room they rented. S18, Ep2
A 14-year-old boy is accused of shooting out a woman's car windshield with a pellet gun. The boy denies the charge. S18, Ep3
A driver delivering honey is sued by her boss for taking the company car without permission and wrecking it. S18, Ep4
